interpretation: Matched same-symbol underlying controls did not confirm a robust incremental 10d path-expansion edge for complete-evidence option abnormality windows.

Bullish_activity, which looked positive in the raw thorough matrix, lost its incremental advantage versus matched controls: 10d path-range delta -0.10 percentage points and 10d directional delta -0.98 percentage points on a window-weighted basis.

Bearish_activity also did not show path expansion versus controls: 10d path-range delta -0.34 percentage points; its 10d directional delta was mildly positive (+0.78 percentage points) but not enough to rescue the broader bearish failure and it was not a path-expansion result.
The current evidence supports descriptive/diagnostic usefulness, not a proven incremental abnormality-to-price relationship suitable for model-layer promotion.
The next proof step, if continued, must use stricter no-option-abnormality control verification and/or regression controls for market, sector, ordinary volatility, liquidity, and prior trend.

matching_policy.control_pool: same-symbol underlying daily bars excluding tested event dates; provider events were not queried for control dates
matching_policy.future_label_leakage: controls are selected without forward returns; forward returns are labels only

promotion_boundary: Diagnostic only. Controls are same-symbol matched underlying windows, but control dates were not separately verified as no-option-abnormality dates and no market/sector/base-feature regression was run.
